steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence structure is: "Aslanian's macroanalysis [blank] nearly 1,000 book titles published between 1512 and 1800 shows not only..." The subject is "Aslanian's macroanalysis" and the main verb is "shows." The blank fills a modifier slot between the subject and the phrase "nearly 1,000 book titles," so it must not create a second competing main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — had examined: A finite past-perfect verb — creates two uncoordinated main verbs ("had examined" and "shows") in one clause. ✗
  • Choice B — examining: A present participle — modifies "macroanalysis" without acting as a finite verb, so "shows" remains the sentence's only main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — examined: A finite past verb — creates two uncoordinated main verbs. ✗
  • Choice D — examines: A finite present verb — creates two uncoordinated main verbs. ✗
B is correct.
